{{Quote|Enjoy the show. The band is playing a cover of a popular song from Tatooine.|[[Mira T'rani]], to a spacer|<ref name="SWG:TPS" />}}
'''Mako''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Musician/Legends|musician]] who lived during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. After the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], he was part of a [[Unidentified Bright Jewel system band|band]] that performed in the [[Bright Jewel system/Legends|Bright Jewel system]], playing the [[Nalargon/Legends|nalargon]]. The band also included two other musicians, [[Alisia (Human)|Alisia]] and [[Alitra]], and two dancers, [[O'choa Detera]] and [[Mestral]]. During that time, the band often played music and danced for the patrons of the [[Cantina/Legends|cantinas]] of [[Tansarii Point Station/Legends|Tansarii Point Station]]<ref name="SWG:TPS" /> and [[Nova Orion Station]], two [[Space station/Legends|space station]] located near the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Ord Mantell/Legends|Ord Mantell]], in the Bright Jewel system.<ref name="SWG:NOS">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=other|Nova Orion Station}}</ref> The band was known to cover popular songs from [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], including [[Mad About Me/Legends|"Mad About Me"]], [[Dune Sea Special/Legends|"Dune Sea Special"]]<ref name="SWG:TPS" /> and [[Lapti Nek/Legends|"Lapti Nek"]].<ref name="SWG:NOS" /> Although Mako and the musicians were very skilled,<ref name="SWG:TPS" /> their lack of original songs sometimes drew criticism.<ref name="SWG:NOS" />

==Behind the scenes==
Mako was a non-player character in the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]'', a [[Wikipedia:Massively multiplayer online role-playing game|massively multiplayer online roleplaying game]] developed by [[Sony]] Online Entertainment and published by [[LucasArts]],<ref name="SWG:TPS" /> prior to its closure on [[December 15]], 2011.<ref name="Shutdown">{{SonyCite|url=playerinfo|text=IMPORTANT INFORMATION ABOUT ''STAR WARS'' GALAXIES™}}</ref> Mako and his band were added to the game as part of the "[[W:c:swg:New Player Tutorial|New Player Tutorial]],"<ref name="SWG:TPS" /> introduced with the release of the "[[W:c:swg:Publish 25 Notes for November 15, 2005|Publish 25]]," a.k.a. the "[[W:c:swg:New Game Enhancements|New Game Enhancements]]," on [[November 15]], [[2005]].<ref name="SonyCite-Publish Archive 2005">{{SonyCite|url=players/content.vm?id=64886&resource=publish|text=Publish Archive 2005}}</ref> The "New Player Tutorial" featured the band in the cantina of [[W:c:swg:Tansarii Point Station|Tansarii Point Station]].<ref name="SWG:TPS" /> With the release of the "[[W:c:swg:Chapter 8 Notes for Jan 15 2008|Chapter 8]]," on [[January 15]], [[2008]],<ref name="SonyCite-Chapter 8: The Nova Orion Crisis">{{SonyCite|url=players/news_archive.vm?id=67563&month=012008|text=Chapter 8: The Nova Orion Crisis}}</ref> Mako and the band were also included in the cantina of the [[W:c:swg:Nova Orion Station|Nova Orion Station]].<ref name="SWG:NOS" />
